sections.html: Your grade reflects professional competency, not just completion. We use a two-part system for Engagement Packets: additive scoring for quality, plus deductions for missing documentation requirements.

sections.label: Category Weights
paragraphs:
CategoryWeightComponents
Foundation Labs30%9 Foundation Labs (quiz-verified)
Engagement Packets45%5 Engagement Packets (Lab X.3, manually graded)
Module Exams25%End-of-module exams (5 total)

sections.label: Engagement Packet Grading: Part 1 — Quality Ladder (Additive)
paragraphs:
ComponentWeightWhat We Evaluate
Assignment Objectives40%All units complete? Timesheet, Runbook, and Client Report present?
Technical Documentation40%Reproducibility — could another analyst follow your runbook?
Professional Communication20%Client-ready? Clear writing, proper formatting, executive summary?

left.label: Part 2 — Documentation Deductions
items: −5% per missing citation (10 required; capped at −50%)
−2.5% per missing screenshot (20 required; a screenshot without the VM name visible counts as missing; capped at −50%)

Citations must meet the source quality standard: NIST, OWASP, RFCs, tool documentation, man pages. Wikipedia, blogs, and tutorial sites do not count toward the 10. See the Citations page for the full rule.
These are not optional. In professional security work, undocumented claims are worthless.

right.label: Drop Policies
items: 1 Foundation Lab grade dropped (lowest of 9)
Lab 1.1 is technically droppable, but in practice it isn’t — every lab after it needs your Kali VM running. Save your drop for a lab that doesn’t gate everything that follows it.
1 Engagement Packet grade dropped (lowest of 5)
1 Module Exam grade dropped (lowest of 5)

sections.label: Think of drops as built-in sick days:
sections.html: Just like at work, sick days are not unlimited. Depending on how long you've been with the company, you get more — but in this class, you only have one per category. Take more than one day at work and it comes out of your check in the form of forfeited wages. Take more than one drop here and it comes out of your GPA in the form of forfeited grades.

sections.label: Late Work Policy:
sections.html: No late work accepted. If an emergency makes you miss a deadline, your one drop in that category absorbs it. Plan accordingly — drops are scarce, so save them for real emergencies.
Questions about grading? Ask in #course-questions on Discord. Document everything — evidence is everything in this field.
